=== Events ===
* In {{ill|Guangxu|ja|光緒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangxu|1=Guangxu Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135852}} 27th Year and {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 34th Year (1901 AD) – The {{ill|Beijing Protocol|ja|辛丑和約|lt=Beijing Protocol|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beijing Protocol|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北京議定書|1=Boxer Protocol|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q672556}}, concerning the Boxer Rebellion (excluding Russia) among a coalition of 11 countries (including Austria, Britain, and France) and the Qing Empire, was signed.
==Months of Yin Metal Ox==
In a Western calendar year whose last digit is either 1 or 6 (corresponding to the Heavenly Stems {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} and {{ill|Bing|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|qq=}}), the month of December becomes the Yin Metal Ox month. (Note that here “month” may refer either to the month of the old calendar or to a seasonal month defined from {{ill|Xiaohan|en|Xiaohan|WD=Q1190989|lt=Xiaohan}} until the day before {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun}}.)

==Days of Yin Metal Ox==
=== 選日 ===
The Yin Metal Ox day is the 9th day of {{ill|Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja|天一天上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja_comment=天一神|1=Tenitsu-shin|2=ja|3=天一神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442153}}.

==Months of Yin Metal Ox==
In a Western calendar year whose last digit is either 1 or 6 (corresponding to the Heavenly Stems {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} and {{ill|Bing|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|qq=}}), the month of December becomes the Yin Metal Ox month. (Note that here “month” may refer either to the month of the old calendar or to a seasonal month defined from {{ill|Xiaohan|en|Xiaohan|WD=Q1190989|lt=Xiaohan}} until the day before {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun}}.)

==Days of Yin Metal Ox==
=== 選日 ===
The Yin Metal Ox day is the 9th day of {{ill|Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja|天一天上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja_comment=天一神|1=Tenitsu-shin|2=ja|3=天一神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442153}}.

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yin Metal Ox|kanji=辛丑|romaji=kanoto ushi|onyomi=shinchū|p=xīnchǒu|w=hsin-ch'ou|mi=ɕin⁵³ ʈʂʰoʊ̯|y=san1 chau2|j=san1 chau2|ci=sɐn tsʰɐu̯|mc=sin tʂʰjuw|oc-bs=sin ʈʂʰuʔ|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}
'''Yin Metal Ox''' (辛丑) is one of the elements of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

It is the 38th combination in the Sexagenary cycle, preceded by {{ill|Gengzi|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|lt=Gengzi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gengzi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and followed by {{ill|Renyin|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|lt=Renyin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renyin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the Heavenly Stem {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} represents yin metal and the Earthly Branch {{ill|Chou|ja|丑|1=chǒu (丑)|lt=Ox (丑)|2=ja|3=丑|1=Ox (zodiac)|lt=Ox (丑)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} represents yin earth; according to the theory of mutual generation (土生金) in the Five Elements philosophy, earth generates metal.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yin Metal Ox (辛丑)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnchǒu
** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 cau2
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-ushi
***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-ushi
***Onyomi 1: shinchu
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinchuk (신축)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Sửu

***Onyomi: しんちゅう (shinchū)

In a Western calendar year whose last digit is either 1 or 6 (corresponding to the Heavenly Stems {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} and {{ill|Bing|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|qq=}}), the month of December becomes the Yin Metal Ox month. (Note that here “month” may refer either to the month of the old calendar or to a seasonal month defined from {{ill|Xiaohan|en|Xiaohan|WD=Q1190989|lt=Xiaohan}} until the day before {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun}}.)

'''Yin Metal Ox''' (辛丑) is one of the elements of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

It is the 38th combination in the Sexagenary cycle, preceded by {{ill|Gengzi|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|lt=Gengzi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gengzi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and followed by {{ill|Renyin|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|lt=Renyin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renyin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the Heavenly Stem {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} represents yin metal and the Earthly Branch {{ill|Chou|ja|丑|1=chǒu (丑)|lt=Ox (丑)|2=ja|3=丑|1=Ox (zodiac)|lt=Ox (丑)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} represents yin earth; according to the theory of mutual generation (土生金) in the Five Elements philosophy, earth generates metal.

=== Events ===
* In {{ill|Guangxu|ja|光緒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangxu|1=Guangxu Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135852}} 27th Year and {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 34th Year (1901 AD) – The {{ill|Beijing Protocol|ja|辛丑和約|lt=Beijing Protocol|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beijing Protocol|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北京議定書|1=Boxer Protocol|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q672556}}, concerning the Boxer Rebellion (excluding Russia) among a coalition of 11 countries (including Austria, Britain, and France) and the Qing Empire, was signed.
==Months of Yin Metal Ox==
In a Western calendar year whose last digit is either 1 or 6 (corresponding to the Heavenly Stems {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} and {{ill|Bing|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|qq=}}), the month of December becomes the Yin Metal Ox month. (Note that here “month” may refer either to the month of the old calendar or to a seasonal month defined from {{ill|Xiaohan|en|Xiaohan|WD=Q1190989|lt=Xiaohan}} until the day before {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun}}.)

==Days of Yin Metal Ox==
=== 選日 ===
The Yin Metal Ox day is the 9th day of {{ill|Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja|天一天上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja_comment=天一神|1=Tenitsu-shin|2=ja|3=天一神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442153}}.

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yin Metal Ox|kanji=辛丑|romaji=kanoto ushi|onyomi=shinchū|p=xīnchǒu|w=hsin-ch'ou|mi=ɕin⁵³ ʈʂʰoʊ̯|y=san1 chau2|j=san1 chau2|ci=sɐn tsʰɐu̯|mc=sin tʂʰjuw|oc-bs=sin ʈʂʰuʔ|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}
'''Yin Metal Ox''' (辛丑) is one of the elements of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

It is the 38th combination in the Sexagenary cycle, preceded by {{ill|Gengzi|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|lt=Gengzi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gengzi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and followed by {{ill|Renyin|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|lt=Renyin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renyin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the Heavenly Stem {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} represents yin metal and the Earthly Branch {{ill|Chou|ja|丑|1=chǒu (丑)|lt=Ox (丑)|2=ja|3=丑|1=Ox (zodiac)|lt=Ox (丑)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} represents yin earth; according to the theory of mutual generation (土生金) in the Five Elements philosophy, earth generates metal.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yin Metal Ox (辛丑)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnchǒu
** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 cau2
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-ushi
***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-ushi
***Onyomi 1: shinchu
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinchuk (신축)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Sửu

***Onyomi: しんかのうし (shinka-no-ushi)
***Onyomi: しんちゅう (shinchū)
** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinchuk
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Sửu

=== Events ===
* In {{ill|Guangxu|ja|光緒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangxu|1=Guangxu Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135852}} 27th Year and {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 34th Year (1901 AD) – The {{ill|Beijing Protocol|ja|辛丑和約|lt=Beijing Protocol|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beijing Protocol|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北京議定書|1=Boxer Protocol|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q672556}}, concerning the Boxer Rebellion (excluding Russia) among a coalition of 11 countries (including Austria, Britain, and France) and the Qing Empire, was signed.
==Months of Yin Metal Ox==
In a Western calendar year whose last digit is either 1 or 6 (corresponding to the Heavenly Stems {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} and {{ill|Bing|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|qq=}}), the month of December becomes the Yin Metal Ox month. (Note that here “month” may refer either to the month of the old calendar or to a seasonal month defined from {{ill|Xiaohan|en|Xiaohan|WD=Q1190989|lt=Xiaohan|1=Xiaohan}} until the day before {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun|1=Lichun}}.)

==Days of Yin Metal Ox==
=== 選日 ===
The Yin Metal Ox day is the 9th day of {{ill|Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja|天一天上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja_comment=天一神|1=Tenitsu-shin|2=ja|3=天一神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442153}}.

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yin Metal Ox|kanji=辛丑|romaji=kanoto ushi|onyomi=shinchū|p=xīnchǒu|w=hsin-ch'ou|mi=ɕin⁵³ ʈʂʰoʊ̯|y=san1 chau2|j=san1 chau2|ci=sɐn tsʰɐu̯|mc=sin tʂʰjuw|oc-bs=sin ʈʂʰuʔ|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}
'''Yin Metal Ox''' (辛丑) is one of the elements of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

It is the 38th combination in the Sexagenary cycle, preceded by {{ill|Gengzi|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|lt=Gengzi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gengzi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and followed by {{ill|Renyin|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|lt=Renyin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renyin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the Heavenly Stem {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} represents yin metal and the Earthly Branch {{ill|Chou|ja|丑|1=chǒu|lt=Ox (丑)|2=ja|3=丑|1=Ox (zodiac)|lt=Ox (丑)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} represents yin earth; according to the theory of mutual generation (土生金) in the Five Elements philosophy, earth generates metal.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yin Metal Ox (辛丑)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnchǒu
** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 cau2
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-ushi
***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-ushi
***Onyomi 1: shinchu
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinchuk (신축)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Sửu

'''Yin Metal Ox''' (辛丑) is one of the elements of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

It is the 38th combination in the Sexagenary cycle, preceded by {{ill|Gengzi|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|lt=Gengzi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gengzi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and followed by {{ill|Renyin|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|lt=Renyin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renyin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the Heavenly Stem {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} represents yin metal and the Earthly Branch {{ill|Chou|ja|丑|1=chǒu|lt=Ox (丑)|2=ja|3=丑|1=Ox (zodiac)|lt=Ox (丑)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} represents yin earth; according to the theory of mutual generation (土生金) in the Five Elements philosophy, earth generates metal.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yin Metal Ox (辛丑)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnchǒu
** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 cau2
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-ushi
***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-ushi
***Onyomi 1: shinchu
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinchuk (신축)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Sửu

=== Events ===
* In {{ill|Guangxu|ja|光緒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guangxu|1=Guangxu Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135852}} 27th Year and {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 34th Year (1901 AD) – The {{ill|Beijing Protocol|ja|辛丑和約|lt=Beijing Protocol|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beijing Protocol|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北京議定書|1=Boxer Protocol|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q672556}}, concerning the Boxer Rebellion (excluding Russia) among a coalition of 11 countries (including Austria, Britain, and France) and the Qing Empire, was signed.
==Months of Yin Metal Ox==
In a Western calendar year whose last digit is either 1 or 6 (corresponding to the Heavenly Stems {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} and {{ill|Bing|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|qq=}}), the month of December becomes the Yin Metal Ox month. (Note that here “month” may refer either to the month of the old calendar or to a seasonal month defined from {{ill|Xiaohan|en|Xiaohan|WD=Q1190989|lt=Xiaohan|1=Xiaohan}} until the day before {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun|1=Lichun}}.)

==Days of Yin Metal Ox==
=== 選日 ===
The Yin Metal Ox day is the 9th day of {{ill|Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja|天一天上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian Yi Tian Shang|ja_comment=天一神|1=Tenitsu-shin|2=ja|3=天一神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442153}}.

'''Yin Metal Ox''' (辛丑) is one of the elements of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

It is the 38th combination in the Sexagenary cycle, preceded by {{ill|Gengzi|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|lt=Gengzi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gengzi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and followed by {{ill|Renyin|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|lt=Renyin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renyin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Five Elements philosophy|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the Heavenly Stem {{ill|Xin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto (辛)|qq=}} represents yin metal and the Earthly Branch {{ill|Chou|ja|丑|1=chǒu|lt=Ox (丑)|2=ja|3=丑|1=Ox (zodiac)|lt=Ox (丑)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} represents yin earth; according to the theory of mutual generation (土生金) in the Five Elements philosophy, earth generates metal.
